title: Login page with email and password
description: As a customer, I want to log in with my email and password so that I can access my accounts securely.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a registered customer
When they enter valid email and password
Then they are prompted for MFA (second factor)

Given invalid credentials
When the customer submits the login form
Then an error message is shown without revealing which field is incorrect

Given a locked account
When the customer tries to log in
Then a message indicates the account is locked with instructions to unlock

title: MFA setup flow (SMS + Authenticator)
description: As a customer, I want to set up multi-factor authentication so that my account has an additional layer of security.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer who has not set up MFA
When they log in for the first time
Then they are guided through MFA setup before accessing the portal

Given MFA setup
When the customer chooses SMS
Then they enter their phone number, receive a code, and verify it

Given MFA setup
When the customer chooses Authenticator app
Then they scan a QR code and enter the generated TOTP code to verify

Given MFA is configured
When the customer completes setup
Then backup recovery codes are generated and displayed once

title: MFA challenge on login
description: As a customer, I want to verify my identity with a second factor on every login so that unauthorized access is prevented.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer with MFA configured
When they enter valid email and password
Then they are prompted for their configured second factor

Given the correct MFA code
When submitted within 60 seconds
Then the customer is authenticated and redirected to the dashboard

Given an incorrect MFA code
When submitted
Then an error is shown and the customer can retry (max 3 attempts before lockout)

title: Biometric login on mobile
description: As a mobile customer, I want to log in using Face ID or fingerprint so that I can access my accounts quickly and securely.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer using iOS with Face ID enabled
When they open the app
Then they can authenticate using Face ID instead of email/password

Given a customer using Android with fingerprint enabled
When they open the app
Then they can authenticate using fingerprint

Given biometric is unavailable (e.g., wet fingers, mask)
When authentication fails
Then the customer falls back to email/password + MFA

Given biometric is not set up on the device
When the customer opens the app
Then they log in with email/password + MFA and are prompted to enable biometric

title: Session timeout and concurrent device limits
description: As a security officer, I want customer sessions to expire after inactivity and limit concurrent sessions so that unauthorized access risk is minimized.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given an active session
When no user activity occurs for 15 minutes
Then the session expires and the customer is redirected to login

Given a customer logged in on 3 devices
When they attempt to log in on a 4th device
Then the oldest session is terminated automatically

Given a session about to expire
When 1 minute remains
Then a warning modal appears with option to extend the session

title: Re-authentication for sensitive operations
description: As a customer, I want to re-verify my identity before performing sensitive operations so that my account is protected even if someone accesses my active session.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given an authenticated customer
When they initiate a transfer > $500
Then they must re-enter their MFA code before proceeding

Given an authenticated customer
When they attempt to change their email, phone, or password
Then they must re-authenticate with MFA

Given re-authentication
When the MFA code is verified
Then a 5-minute elevated session is granted (no re-auth needed for additional sensitive ops within window)

title: Password reset with MFA verification
description: As a customer, I want to reset my password securely so that I can regain access if I forget my credentials.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer on the login page
When they click 'Forgot Password'
Then they enter their email and receive a reset link (valid 30 minutes)

Given a valid reset link
When the customer clicks it
Then they must verify via MFA before setting a new password

Given a new password
When submitted
Then it must meet complexity requirements (8+ chars, upper, lower, number, special)
And all existing sessions are invalidated

title: Force logout from all devices
description: As a customer, I want to log out of all devices from my profile so that I can secure my account if I suspect compromise.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer on the profile page
When they click 'Log out of all devices'
Then all active sessions and tokens are invalidated
And the customer is redirected to login

Given sessions are invalidated
When any other device makes a request
Then it receives a 401 and redirects to login

title: Account lockout after failed login attempts
description: As a security officer, I want accounts to lock after repeated failed login attempts so that brute force attacks are prevented.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer account
When 3 failed login attempts occur within 10 minutes
Then a warning notification is sent (email + SMS)

Given 5 failed login attempts
When the threshold is reached
Then the account is locked for 30 minutes

Given a locked account
When 30 minutes elapse
Then the account auto-unlocks
OR the customer can unlock via email verification + MFA

Given 10 failed attempts in 24 hours
When the threshold is reached
Then the account is locked permanently until customer calls support

title: Security headers and OWASP compliance
description: As a security officer, I want all responses to include appropriate security headers so that common web vulnerabilities are mitigated.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given any HTTP response from the portal
Then the following headers are present:
- Content-Security-Policy (strict, no inline scripts)
- Strict-Transport-Security (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains)
- X-Frame-Options: DENY
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

Given any API endpoint
When rate limit is exceeded (10 requests/second per IP)
Then a 429 response is returned with Retry-After header

title: Login audit logging
description: As a compliance officer, I want all authentication events logged so that we have a complete audit trail for regulatory reporting.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given any authentication event (login, logout, MFA, password change, lockout)
Then the following is logged:
- Timestamp (UTC)
- Customer ID
- Event type
- IP address
- User agent
- Outcome (success/failure)
- Geo-location (from IP)

Given audit logs
When stored
Then they are append-only (immutable) and retained for 7 years

Given a compliance request
When audit logs are queried
Then results return within 10 seconds for any 30-day window

title: Account list with balances
description: As a customer, I want to see all my accounts with current balances on the dashboard so that I have a quick overview of my financial position.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given an authenticated customer with multiple accounts
When they land on the dashboard
Then they see a card for each account showing:
- Account nickname (editable)
- Last 4 digits of account number
- Account type (Checking, Savings, Money Market, CD)
- Available balance and ledger balance
- Account status badge (Active/Frozen/Closed)

Given an account with no nickname
When displayed
Then the account type + last 4 digits is used as default label

Given the dashboard
When loading
Then a loading skeleton is shown and data loads within 2 seconds

title: Pending transactions display
description: As a customer, I want to see pending transactions separately so that I understand my available vs ledger balance.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given an account with pending transactions
When the customer views the account
Then pending transactions are shown in a separate section with a 'Pending' badge

Given pending transactions
When displayed
Then they show: date, description, amount, and 'Pending' status
And the total pending amount is shown next to available balance

title: Transaction history with 30-day default view
description: As a customer, I want to see my recent transactions so that I can track my spending and account activity.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given an authenticated customer viewing an account
When they navigate to transaction history
Then the last 30 days of transactions are displayed by default

Given the transaction list
Then each row shows: date, description, category, amount (debit/credit), running balance

Given many transactions
When paginated
Then 25 rows shown by default with option for 50 or 100
And pagination controls at bottom

title: Transaction search and filter (18 months)
description: As a customer, I want to search and filter my transaction history so that I can find specific transactions quickly.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the transaction history page
When the customer applies filters
Then they can filter by:
- Date range (up to 18 months back)
- Amount range (min/max)
- Description keyword (partial match)
- Transaction type (debit, credit, transfer, bill pay)

Given search results
When returned
Then they display within 500ms
And the active filters are shown as removable chips

title: Transaction export (CSV and PDF)
description: As a customer, I want to export my transactions so that I can use them for budgeting or tax purposes.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given filtered transaction results
When the customer clicks 'Export CSV'
Then a CSV file downloads with all visible columns

Given filtered transaction results
When the customer clicks 'Export PDF'
Then a formatted PDF generates with account info header, date range, and transaction table

Given a large export (>10,000 rows)
When requested
Then a progress indicator shows and the file is generated server-side

title: Transfer between own accounts
description: As a customer, I want to transfer money between my own accounts so that I can manage my funds across accounts.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer with 2+ accounts
When they select source and destination accounts and enter an amount
Then the transfer processes in real-time
And both account balances update immediately

Given a transfer amount exceeding available balance
When submitted
Then an error states 'Insufficient funds' and the transfer is blocked

Given a successful transfer
Then a confirmation shows: amount, from account, to account, timestamp, confirmation number

title: Transfer to another Contoso customer
description: As a customer, I want to send money to other Contoso customers so that I can pay friends, family, or businesses banking with Contoso.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer initiating a transfer
When they enter a recipient's account + routing number (or select saved payee)
Then the system validates the recipient exists

Given a valid transfer
When submitted during business hours (6 AM – 9 PM ET)
Then it processes in real-time

Given a valid transfer
When submitted outside business hours
Then it is queued for next business day with 'Pending' status

Given the transfer amount
When it exceeds the per-transaction limit ($2,500)
Then the transfer is blocked with error message showing the limit

title: Transfer limits enforcement
description: As a risk officer, I want transfer limits enforced so that financial exposure is capped and fraud risk is reduced.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given any transfer to another customer
When the per-transaction amount exceeds $2,500
Then the transfer is rejected

Given transfers throughout the day
When the cumulative daily total exceeds $5,000
Then additional transfers are rejected until the next calendar day (ET)

Given a transfer is rejected due to limits
Then the error message shows: current limit, daily total so far, and remaining allowance

Given account-type-specific limits configured by back-office
When they differ from defaults
Then the custom limits apply

title: Cancel pending transfer
description: As a customer, I want to cancel a pending transfer so that I can correct mistakes before the transfer processes.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a pending (unprocessed) transfer
When the customer clicks 'Cancel'
Then a confirmation dialog appears

Given cancellation is confirmed
Then the transfer status changes to 'Cancelled'
And the held amount is released back to available balance

Given a processed transfer
When the customer tries to cancel
Then the cancel button is disabled with tooltip: 'Completed transfers cannot be cancelled. Contact support.'

title: Payee CRUD operations
description: As a customer, I want to save, edit, and delete payees so that I don't have to re-enter recipient details for each transfer.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the payee management page
When the customer adds a new payee
Then they enter: name, account number, routing number, and optional nickname

Given a saved payee
When the customer edits it
Then they can update the nickname and account details

Given a saved payee
When the customer deletes it
Then a confirmation dialog appears and the payee is removed from the list

Given the payee list
Then it is searchable by name/nickname and sortable alphabetically

title: New payee 24-hour hold
description: As a risk officer, I want a 24-hour hold on first transfers to newly added payees so that customers have time to detect and report compromised accounts.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a newly added payee
When the customer initiates the first transfer within 24 hours of adding
Then the transfer is held for 24 hours before processing
And a notification is sent (email + in-app) explaining the hold

Given the 24-hour hold period has elapsed
When subsequent transfers are made to the same payee
Then they process normally without holds

title: Re-authentication for transfers over $500
description: As a customer, I want to confirm my identity for large transfers so that my account is protected from unauthorized transactions.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a transfer > $500
When the customer submits the transfer form
Then an MFA challenge is presented before processing

Given the MFA is verified
Then the transfer proceeds
And a 5-minute elevated session allows additional large transfers without re-auth

Given the MFA fails
Then the transfer is cancelled and a security notification is sent

title: Fraud velocity checks
description: As a risk officer, I want velocity checks on transfers so that suspiciously rapid transaction patterns are blocked.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a customer making transfers
When more than 5 transfers are initiated within 10 minutes
Then the 6th transfer is blocked
And a security alert is triggered (email + in-app)

Given a velocity block
When the customer waits 10 minutes
Then transfers are re-enabled

Given repeated velocity blocks (3+ in 24 hours)
Then the account is flagged for manual review by fraud team

title: Transfer confirmation and receipt
description: As a customer, I want to receive a confirmation and receipt after each transfer so that I have proof of the transaction.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a successful transfer
Then a confirmation screen shows:
- Confirmation number
- Amount
- From/To accounts
- Date and time
- Status (Completed/Pending)

Given a successful transfer
Then an email receipt is sent to the customer's registered email

Given the confirmation screen
When the customer clicks 'Print Receipt'
Then a printer-friendly version is generated

title: Transfer history and search
description: As a customer, I want to view and search my transfer history so that I can track past transfers.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the transfer history page
Then all transfers (completed, pending, cancelled, failed) are listed

Given the transfer list
When the customer applies filters
Then they can filter by: date, amount, recipient, status

Given transfer history
When exported
Then a CSV file downloads with all transfer details

title: Biller search and directory
description: As a customer, I want to search for billers so that I can quickly set up bill payments.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the bill pay setup page
When the customer types in the biller search field
Then typeahead suggestions appear within 300ms

Given search results
When the customer selects a biller
Then the biller's details (name, address) auto-populate
And the customer only needs to enter their account number with that biller

title: Manual biller entry
description: As a customer, I want to manually enter biller details when my biller isn't in the directory.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the bill pay setup page
When the customer selects 'Add manually'
Then they enter: biller name, mailing address, account number

Given the manual entry form
When submitted with valid data
Then the biller is saved to the customer's biller list

Given invalid data (missing required fields)
Then validation errors are shown inline

title: One-time bill payment
description: As a customer, I want to make a one-time bill payment so that I can pay a bill on a specific date.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a saved biller
When the customer sets amount, payment date, and source account
Then the payment is scheduled

Given a payment date
When it is today and before 4 PM ET
Then it is processed same-day

Given a payment date
When it is today after 4 PM ET
Then it is processed next business day

Given the minimum payment
When the amount is less than $1.00
Then validation prevents submission

title: Payment history
description: As a customer, I want to see my bill payment history so that I can confirm payments were made.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the bill pay section
When the customer views payment history
Then all payments are listed with: date, biller, amount, status, confirmation number

Given payment statuses
Then they include: Scheduled, Processing, Completed, Failed, Cancelled

title: Recurring payment setup
description: As a customer, I want to set up recurring bill payments so that my bills are paid automatically.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a saved biller
When the customer selects 'Recurring'
Then they choose: frequency (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, quarterly), start date, amount, source account

Given a recurring payment is set up
Then the next payment date is calculated and displayed

Given frequency = monthly
When the start date is the 31st
Then the system handles short months (uses last day of month)

title: Recurring payment management
description: As a customer, I want to edit, pause, and cancel recurring payments so that I can adjust as my needs change.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a recurring payment
When the customer edits it
Then they can change: amount, frequency, next payment date, source account

Given a recurring payment
When the customer pauses it
Then they set a resume date and no payments are processed until then

Given a recurring payment
When the customer cancels it
Then a confirmation dialog appears and future payments are stopped
And the cancellation is logged in payment history

title: Bill payment reminders
description: As a customer, I want to receive reminders before scheduled payments so that I can ensure sufficient funds.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a scheduled payment (one-time or recurring)
When 3 days remain before the payment date
Then an email reminder is sent with: biller name, amount, date, source account

Given insufficient funds on payment date
When the payment fails
Then a notification is sent immediately with instructions to reschedule

title: Support ticket submission
description: As a customer, I want to submit a support ticket so that I can get help without calling.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the support page
When the customer submits a ticket
Then they fill in: category (dropdown), subject, description
And optionally attach up to 3 files (10 MB each, PDF/JPG/PNG)

Given a submitted ticket
Then a ticket number is auto-generated and displayed
And a confirmation email is sent with the ticket number and expected response time

title: Ticket list and status tracking
description: As a customer, I want to see all my support tickets and their statuses so that I know what's being worked on.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the support page
When the customer views their tickets
Then they see a list with: ticket #, subject, category, status, created date, last updated

Given ticket statuses
Then they display with color-coded badges: Submitted (blue), In Review (yellow), Awaiting Customer (orange), Resolved (green), Closed (gray)

Given many tickets
Then the list is searchable and filterable by status and date

title: Ticket detail and comment thread
description: As a customer, I want to view ticket details and add comments so that I can communicate with the support team.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a ticket detail page
Then it shows: all original details, attachments, and a comment thread

Given an open ticket
When the customer adds a comment
Then it appears in the thread with timestamp and 'Customer' badge

Given a new comment from support
Then a notification is sent and the comment appears with 'Support' badge

title: Reopen resolved ticket within 7 days
description: As a customer, I want to reopen a resolved ticket if my issue isn't actually fixed.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a ticket with status 'Resolved'
When within 7 days of resolution
Then a 'Reopen' button is visible

Given the customer clicks 'Reopen'
Then the status changes to 'Submitted' and the support team is notified

Given a ticket resolved > 7 days ago
Then the 'Reopen' button is hidden and a message says 'Please submit a new ticket'

title: SLA display by ticket category
description: As a customer, I want to see expected response times so that I know when to expect a reply.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given ticket submission
When a category is selected
Then the expected response time is shown:
- Account inquiry: 1 business day
- Transaction dispute: 2 business hours
- Technical issue: 4 business hours
- Document request: 1 business day
- Other: 2 business days

Given a submitted ticket
Then the SLA countdown is visible on the ticket detail page

title: File upload with drag and drop
description: As a customer, I want to upload documents by dragging and dropping or browsing so that I can fulfill document requests easily.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the document upload area
When the customer drags a file over it
Then a visual drop zone indicator appears

Given a dropped or browsed file
When the file type is PDF, JPG, PNG, or TIFF and size is ≤ 25 MB
Then the file is accepted and upload begins with a progress bar

Given an unsupported file type or oversized file
Then an error message is shown immediately (client-side validation)

Given multiple files in one request
When total size exceeds 100 MB
Then additional files are rejected with an error

title: Virus scanning on upload
description: As a security officer, I want all uploaded files scanned for viruses so that malicious files don't enter our systems.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a file upload
When the file reaches the server
Then it is scanned for viruses before being stored

Given a clean file
Then it is stored and the upload status shows 'Complete'

Given an infected file
Then it is rejected, the upload status shows 'Blocked — Threat Detected'
And the customer sees: 'This file was blocked for security reasons. Please scan your device and try a different file.'

Given scan processing
When it takes > 5 seconds
Then a 'Scanning...' status is shown to the customer

title: Document request workflow
description: As a customer, I want to see what documents have been requested and upload against specific requests.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a back-office document request
When it is created
Then the customer sees it in their 'Document Requests' section with:
- Document type needed
- Reason for request
- Deadline

Given a document request
When the customer uploads a file
Then it is linked to that specific request
And the request status changes from 'Requested' to 'Uploaded'

title: Document status tracking
description: As a customer, I want to track the status of my uploaded documents so that I know if they've been accepted.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given an uploaded document
Then its status progresses through: Requested → Uploaded → Under Review → Accepted/Rejected

Given a status change
Then a notification is sent to the customer

Given a rejected document
Then the reason is displayed and the customer can re-upload

title: Document history with 7-year view
description: As a customer, I want to view my previously uploaded documents so that I have access to my submission history.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the document history page
Then all documents uploaded in the last 7 years are listed

Given a document in history
When the customer clicks to view
Then the document opens in a viewer (not download) for security

Given document history
Then documents cannot be deleted by the customer
And the list is searchable by type, date, and status

title: Email notification service
description: As a customer, I want to receive email notifications for account activity so that I stay informed.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a notification event
When email is the selected channel
Then an email is sent using the appropriate template (branded, responsive)

Given email delivery
Then delivery status is logged (sent, delivered, bounced)

Given email notifications
Then they include a 'Manage notifications' link to preferences page

Given regulatory emails
Then they include required legal footer and no unsubscribe for mandatory notifications

title: In-app notification center
description: As a customer, I want an in-app notification center so that I see important updates without checking email.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the portal header
Then a bell icon shows the count of unread notifications

Given the notification dropdown
When clicked
Then it shows the 10 most recent notifications with: icon, title, time ago

Given a notification
When clicked
Then it marks as read and navigates to the relevant page

Given the 'View All' link
When clicked
Then a full notification history page opens with search and filters

title: Mandatory notification triggers
description: As a security officer, I want certain notifications to always be sent regardless of customer preferences.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given mandatory events:
- Login from new device/location
- Password change
- 3+ failed login attempts
- Transfer > $1,000
- Account status change (frozen, closed)

When any mandatory event occurs
Then notifications are sent via ALL active channels (email + in-app minimum)
And the customer cannot disable these notifications

title: Notification preferences UI
description: As a customer, I want to choose which notifications I receive and how so that I'm not overwhelmed with alerts.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the notification preferences page
Then a matrix is shown: rows = notification categories, columns = channels (email, in-app)

Given mandatory notifications
Then their toggles are locked ON with a lock icon and tooltip: 'Required for account security'

Given optional notifications
When the customer toggles a channel off
Then future notifications of that type are not sent via that channel

Given threshold-based notifications (transaction alerts, low balance)
Then the customer can set custom threshold amounts

title: Notification preference enforcement
description: As a customer, I want my notification preferences to be respected so that I only receive the alerts I've opted into.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given a notification event
When the notification service processes it
Then it checks the customer's preferences before sending

Given a channel is disabled for a category
Then no notification is sent via that channel

Given a mandatory notification
Then preference checks are bypassed and the notification is always sent

title: Notification history
description: As a customer, I want to see a history of all notifications sent to me so that I can review past alerts.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the notification history page
Then all notifications (read and unread) are listed chronologically

Given each notification
Then it shows: type icon, title, message preview, timestamp, channel(s), read/unread status

Given the list
Then it is filterable by: type, date range, read/unread status

title: View profile page
description: As a customer, I want to view my profile information so that I can verify my details are correct.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the profile page
Then it shows:
- Editable: email, phone, mailing address, display name
- Non-editable: legal name, SSN (masked), DOB, account type

Given non-editable fields
Then they show a lock icon and 'Contact support to update' message

title: Edit contact info with re-verification
description: As a customer, I want to update my email and phone with verification so that my contact details are accurate and secure.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given email change
When the customer enters a new email
Then a verification link is sent to the NEW email
And the change takes effect only after clicking the link

Given phone number change
When the customer enters a new phone number
Then an OTP is sent to the NEW number
And the change takes effect only after entering the correct OTP

Given address change
When submitted
Then it updates immediately (no verification needed)
And a confirmation notification is sent

title: Edit preferences and settings
description: As a customer, I want to update my display preferences so that the portal suits my needs.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the preferences section
When the customer updates settings (language, timezone, date format)
Then changes save immediately and reflect across the portal

Given notification preferences
Then a link navigates to the dedicated notification preferences page

title: Change password
description: As a customer, I want to change my password so that I can maintain account security.
acceptanceCriteria:
Given the change password form
When the customer enters current password + new password
Then new password is validated against complexity rules (8+ chars, upper, lower, number, special)

Given a valid new password
When submitted
Then MFA is required before the change takes effect

Given a successful password change
Then all other sessions are invalidated
And a confirmation notification is sent via all channels
